notes: No joins — pure filtered scan with scalar aggregate.
All three predicates fused into single tight loop per block (branch-free).
Column load order: l_shipdate first (most selective at 8%), then l_discount + l_quantity + l_extendedprice together.
Zone map on l_shipdate allows block skipping before loading any other column.

parallelism.notes: Each thread owns a local double revenue accumulator. After all morsels complete, main thread sums 64 doubles — trivial reduction cost.

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][{int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size} x 600]
index_usage.expected_pruning: 1-5% blocks skipped (data unsorted by date — most blocks span full 1992-1998 range)

column_load_order.rationale: Most selective filter (8%). Cheapest dtype (int32). Eliminates 92% of rows before loading doubles.
Second most selective (10% of remaining). Load only after shipdate passes.
Least selective (46%). Load together with discount to amortize I/O.

column_load_order.action: multiply with l_discount, accumulate into local_revenue
column_load_order.rationale: Only needed for qualifying rows. Loaded in same pass for SIMD fused multiply-add.

operations: Load 8x int32 shipdate, compare >= 8766 and < 9131 → 8-bit mask
Load 8x double discount, compare >= 0.05 and <= 0.07 → AND into mask
Load 8x double quantity, compare < 24.0 → AND into mask

physical_plan.memory_notes: All four column files read sequentially. No random access. Hardware prefetcher fully effective. Total data volume: ~1.68 GB (240 MB shipdate int32 + 3×480 MB doubles).

estimated_cost.notes: With 64 threads and AVX-512, filter+accumulate throughput is not the bottleneck — sequential HDD/memory bandwidth is. Zone map reduces overhead on block boundary checks. Target matches GenDB baseline of 14ms.
